title: South America flight-pricing scratch — USA → Colombia → Brazil routing math
html:
South America flight-pricing scratch — USA → Colombia → Brazil routing math

Source: 2025-04-22 Apple Note. A four-line price-comparison scratch for a hypothetical South America trip. Captured while still in Grants Pass in the Brotein grind period, ~5 months before the Austin move.

TL;DR

  • Routing A (multi-stop): USA → Colombia $250 → Brazil $250 → USA $430 = $930 total.
  • Routing B (round-trip 2 weeks): USA ↔ Brazil $887.
  • The math implicitly favors Routing A only by $43 — and only if the Colombia leg has standalone value.
  • This is the "Carnival Brazil" line item from the May 20 side quests list getting priced one month before it was formally listed.
  • A specimen of Yahir's mode used as research notebook — not a manifesto, not a todo, just spot-pricing a fantasy.

Key quote

(no prose — pure pricing table)

Pattern observed

The South America trip never appears to have happened in the corpus — it gets priced (April), aspirationally listed (May 20), and then Austin eats every dollar that would have funded it. This is a frozen alternative timeline node: what Yahir would have spent his money on if he hadn't moved to Texas.

Open questions

  • Was this sparked by a specific cheap-flight alert, or was it speculative spend-modeling?
  • The implied trip duration (multi-country) doesn't fit two weeks — Routing A reads more like a 2–4 week itinerary.
  • Does this trip ever resurface in 2026 notes?

Related
